title: gh#59: R1.4 — Switch publish-worker to publishers map; drop parallel switches
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/59.
Backlog section: Candidate 1 — Publisher seam.

## Context

Fourth PR in Candidate 1. The wiring change that delivers the locality win: replaces the two parallel platform switches in `publish-worker.ts` with a single map lookup, and removes the `resolvedPlatform` mutable-state coordination workaround.

## Scope

- Modify `packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts`:
  - `PublishHandlerDeps` gains `publishers?: Partial<Record<Platform, Publisher>>` with defaults from the new factories
  - Drop `callTwitterImpl`, `callLinkedInImpl`, `callFacebookImpl` separate overrides (subsumed)
  - Drop `checkLinkedInBudgetImpl`, `checkFacebookBudgetImpl` (untouched by this PR; budget check still callback-shaped — moved unchanged)
  - Drop the `resolvedPlatform: SupportedPlatform` mutable `let` and the six-line flow-analysis cast comment
  - The `publish` callback in `PublishContext` (passed to the lifecycle) becomes `publishers[platform].publish(profile, publishablePost, ctx)`
  - The catch block becomes platform-blind:
    ```ts
    if (err instanceof PostLifecycleAbort) { /* graceful skip */ }
    if (err instanceof PublishFailure) {
      if (err.kind === 'permanent') throw new UnrecoverableError(err.message);
      throw err; // transient — BullMQ retries
    }
    throw err;
    ```
- Modify `packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts`:
  - `PublishContext.callTwitter` renamed to `publish` (or similar — caller-chosen)
  - The `extras` escape hatch is removed; `PublishablePost` carries platform-specific fields directly
  - Lifecycle loads media before constructing `PublishablePost` (text-only posts get `media: []`)
- Update existing publish-worker tests to use `publishers` override (one injection point) instead of three `callXImpl` overrides

## Out of scope

- Removing the old platform services and `error-classifier` (R1.5)
- TokenVault refactor (deferred per ADR-0002)
- Budget-loader dedupe (Candidate #6, independent)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` passes
- All existing worker tests pass with no behavior change
- `rg "resolvedPlatform" packages/worker/` returns nothing
- `rg "classifyTwitterError|classifyLinkedInError|classifyFacebookError" packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts` returns nothing

## References

- ADRs: [0001](../blob/main/docs/adr/0001-publish-failure-is-thrown-not-returned.md), [0002](../blob/main/docs/adr/0002-publisher-receives-raw-profile-row.md)
- Depends on: #57, #58

history.reasoning: The original evidence files still exist, and the requested refactor is not present. packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts still has separate callTwitterImpl/callLinkedInImpl/callFacebookImpl deps, separate budget-check overrides, a mutable resolvedPlatform, a platform switch inside the lifecycle callback, and classifier dispatch in the catch block. The acceptance probes fail: resolvedPlatform and classifyTwitterError|classifyLinkedInError|classifyFacebookError are still present in publish-worker.ts. packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts still exposes PublishContext.callTwitter with an extras argument and invokes ctx.callTwitter rather than a generic publish callback with a PublishablePost. Publisher classes and PublishablePost exist elsewhere, and pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit passes, but the worker/lifecycle wiring has not been switched to the publishers map. Full build/test commands could not complete in this read-only sandbox because they attempted to write dist/.vite-temp files and failed with EPERM, which is environmental and does not change the code evidence.
commands: rg -n "export interface Publisher|type Publisher|create.*Publisher|PublishablePost|PublishFailure" packages/worker/src packages/shared/src packages/shared/lib

history.reasoning: The original evidence files still exist, but the issue no longer reproduces in the current code. packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts now has PublishHandlerDeps.publishers, creates default Twitter/LinkedIn/Facebook publishers, and calls publishers[publishablePost.platform].publish(...) from the lifecycle publish callback. The old callTwitterImpl/callLinkedInImpl/callFacebookImpl overrides, resolvedPlatform mutable state, and publish-worker error classifier dispatch are absent. The catch block is platform-blind for PostLifecycleAbort and PublishFailure. packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.service.ts now exposes PublishContext.publish, constructs a shared PublishablePost after loadPublishableMedia(...), includes platform-specific fields directly, and calls ctx.publish(...). packages/shared/src/publisher.ts defines PublishablePost with media, visibility, and linkUrl. Acceptance rg probes for resolvedPlatform and publish-worker classifiers returned no matches. Typecheck via pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit passed. Full build and vitest could not complete in this read-only sandbox because they attempted to write dist/.vite-temp or temp SSR files and failed with EPERM, which is environmental rather than code evidence.
